perturbation.original_text: 16. TERMINATION A) Either party may terminate this agreement by giving written Notice to the other party if: i) The other party fails to perform or satisfy any of the conditions, covenants or obligations of this Agreement. ii) The other party files or has filed against it, a petition seeking relief under any bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganisation, moratorium, liquidation or similar law affecting creditors' rights B) In addition, Lucid may terminate this agreement by giving the Distributor Written Notice if there is any change of control, ownership or management of the Distributor. C) Either party may terminate this agreement by providing Ninety days Written Notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. TERMINATION A) Either party may terminate this agreement by giving written Notice to the other party if: i) The other party fails to perform or satisfy any of the conditions, covenants or obligations of this Agreement. ii) The other party files or has filed against it, a petition seeking relief under any bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganisation, moratorium, liquidation or similar law affecting creditors' rights B) In addition, Lucid may terminate this agreement by giving the Distributor Written Notice if there is any change of control, ownership or management of the Distributor. C) Either party may terminate this agreement by providing Ninety days Written Notice. D) Lucid may terminate this agreement at any time, without notice or cause, if the Distributor fails to meet the forecasted annual sales.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 16(C) states that either party can terminate the agreement with ninety days written notice. By adding section 16(D), Lucid reserves the right to terminate the agreement immediately and without notice, based on a failure to meet forecasted sales. The lack of a notice period in 16(D) directly conflicts with the ninety-day notice requirement in 16(C), creating uncertainty about termination conditions, which is contradicting another termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. TERMINATION A) Either party may terminate this agreement by giving written Notice to the other party if: i) The other party fails to perform or satisfy any of the conditions, covenants or obligations of this Agreement. ii) The other party files or has filed against it, a petition seeking relief under any bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganisation, moratorium, liquidation or similar law affecting creditors' rights B) In addition, Lucid may terminate this agreement by giving the Distributor Written Notice if there is any change of control, ownership or management of the Distributor. C) Either party may terminate this agreement by providing Ninety days Written Notice. D) Lucid may terminate this agreement at any time, without notice or cause, if the Distributor fails to meet the forecasted annual sales.
perturbation.justification: The addition of section 16(D) to the contract introduces a contradiction with section 16(C). Section 16(C) states that either party can terminate the agreement with ninety days written notice. However, section 16(D) allows Lucid to terminate the agreement immediately and without notice if the Distributor fails to meet forecasted annual sales. This creates a direct conflict regarding the notice period required for termination, as the Distributor would expect a ninety-day notice based on 16(C), while Lucid could terminate immediately under 16(D). This inconsistency regarding the terms of termination creates a strong in-text contradiction, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. Thus, it adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Appointment A) Lucid appoints the Distributor and the Distributor accepts appointment as an exclusive authorized Lucid Distributor. The Distributor will be entitled to purchase certain Lucid products and resell them to End User customers within the following market segments:- hospital and medical research centres, including but not limited to dermatology, pathology, plastic surgery and mohs surgery departments, clinical research centres, small animal research facilities, cosmetic and cosmeceutical companies. B) The relationship of the parties under this Agreement is that of independent contractors and nothing contained herein shall be construed as creating any partnership, joint venture or agency relationship between the Distributor and Lucid. Under no circumstances shall any employees of one party be deemed the employees of the other for any purpose. The Distributor shall not have the authority to assume or create any obligation, or make any representation of any kind on behalf of Lucid.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Appointment A) Lucid appoints the Distributor and the Distributor accepts appointment as an exclusive authorized Lucid Distributor. The Distributor will be entitled to purchase certain Lucid products and resell them to End User customers within the following market segments:- hospital and medical research centres, including but not limited to dermatology, pathology, plastic surgery and mohs surgery departments, clinical research centres, small animal research facilities, cosmetic and cosmeceutical companies. B) The relationship of the parties under this Agreement is that of an agent and nothing contained herein shall be construed as creating any partnership, joint venture or independent contractors relationship between the Distributor and Lucid. Under all circumstances shall any employees of one party be deemed the employees of the other for any purpose. The Distributor shall have the authority to assume or create any obligation, or make any representation of any kind on behalf of Lucid.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of Section 1.B states that the Distributor is an independent contractor and does not have the authority to represent Lucid. By changing this section to state that the Distributor is an agent of Lucid and does have the authority to represent Lucid, a clear contradiction is introduced. The in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty and legal risk, as it is unclear whether the distributor has the power to make deals on behalf of Lucid or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Duties of The Distributor A) The Distributor will use his best efforts to actively promote and sell the Product to all relevant End Users within all relevant market segments within the Territory. B) The Distributor agrees to undertake detailed sales demonstrations of the Product(s) to potential End Users within all relevant market segments within the Territory. C) The Distributor agrees to allow the End User to undertake sales evaluations (trials) of the product(s), using the Distributor's own Demonstration Equipment as necessary. D) The Distributor agrees to promote the Product(s) at all relevant trade shows, seminars and exhibitions held within the Territory. Lucid may, at it's discretion, and in co-ordination with the Distributor when Lucid deems it necessary, at its own expense and in its own name, engage in advertising activities of or hold or participate in exhibitions of the Product in the Territory. E) The Distributors will not develop, manufacture or sell any equipment or service, which in any way can be considered to be competitive to the equipment or service offered by Lucid to the Distributor as Product. Lucid reserves the right to inform the Distributor from time to time of specific products and companies that Lucid considers to be competitive. A competitive product includes, but is not limited to, any product that would infringe any claim of any Lucid owned or licensed patent issued or pending worldwide, whether or not corresponding patent claims are in force in the Distributors territory or the sale of which would restrict sale of Lucid product. F) The Distributor will provide to the End User, full installation and customer training of the Product(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding the distributor's status and authority. Originally, the distributor was an independent contractor with no authority to represent Lucid. The changed text makes the distributor an agent with full authority. This directly contradicts section 5, which outlines the distributor's *duties*. If the distributor is an agent, these are not merely 'duties' but legally binding obligations undertaken on Lucid's behalf, introducing a legal inconsistency. The change creates uncertainty in the enforceability of the agreement with respect to the distributor.
